variants.description: Get a dashboard using the specified ID.

schema.name: author_handle
schema.description: Identifier of the dashboard author.

schema.name: author_name
schema.description: Name of the dashboard author.

schema.name: created_at
schema.description: Creation date of the dashboard.

schema.name: description
schema.description: Description of the dashboard.

schema.name: id
schema.description: ID of the dashboard.

schema.name: is_read_only
schema.description:
Whether this dashboard is read-only. If True, only the author and admins can make changes to it.

This property is deprecated; please use the [Restriction Policies API](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/restriction-policies/) instead to manage write authorization for individual dashboards.

schema.name: layout_type
schema.description: Layout type of the dashboard.

schema.name: modified_at
schema.description: Modification date of the dashboard.

schema.name: notify_list
schema.description: List of handles of users to notify when changes are made to this dashboard.

schema.name: reflow_type
schema.description:
Reflow type for a **new dashboard layout** dashboard. Set this only when layout type is 'ordered'.
If set to 'fixed', the dashboard expects all widgets to have a layout, and if it's set to 'auto',
widgets should not have layouts.

schema.name: restricted_roles
schema.description: A list of role identifiers. Only the author and users associated with at least one of these roles can edit this dashboard.

schema.name: tabs
schema.description: List of tabs for organizing dashboard widgets into groups.

children.name: widget_ids
children.description: List of widget IDs belonging to this tab. The backend also accepts positional references in @N format (1-indexed) as a convenience for Terraform and other declarative tools.

schema.name: tags
schema.description: List of team names representing ownership of a dashboard.

schema.name: template_variable_presets
schema.description: Array of template variables saved views.

children.name: value
children.description: (deprecated) The value of the template variable within the saved view. Cannot be used in conjunction with `values`.

children.name: values
children.description: One or many template variable values within the saved view, which will be unioned together using `OR` if more than one is specified. Cannot be used in conjunction with `value`.

schema.name: template_variables
schema.description: List of template variables for this dashboard.

children.name: available_values
children.description: The list of values that the template variable drop-down is limited to.

children.name: default
children.description: (deprecated) The default value for the template variable on dashboard load. Cannot be used in conjunction with `defaults`.

children.name: defaults
children.description: One or many default values for template variables on load. If more than one default is specified, they will be unioned together with `OR`. Cannot be used in conjunction with `default`.

children.name: prefix
children.description: The tag prefix associated with the variable. Only tags with this prefix appear in the variable drop-down.

children.name: type
children.description: The type of variable. This is to differentiate between filter variables (interpolated in query) and group by variables (interpolated into group by).

schema.name: title
schema.description: Title of the dashboard.

schema.name: url
schema.description: The URL of the dashboard.

schema.name: widgets
schema.description: List of widgets to display on the dashboard.

fields.name: alert_id
fields.description: ID of the alert to use in the widget.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the widget.

fields.name: precision
fields.description: Number of decimal to show. If not defined, will use the raw value.

fields.name: text_align
fields.description: How to align the text on the widget.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the widget.

fields.name: title_align
fields.description: How to align the text on the widget.

fields.name: title_size
fields.description: Size of value in the widget.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of the alert value widget.

fields.name: unit
fields.description: Unit to display with the value.

fields.name: custom_links
fields.description: List of custom links.

children.name: is_hidden
children.description: The flag for toggling context menu link visibility.

children.name: label
children.description: The label for the custom link URL. Keep the label short and descriptive. Use metrics and tags as variables.

children.name: link
children.description: The URL of the custom link. URL must include `http` or `https`. A relative URL must start with `/`.

children.name: override_label
children.description: The label ID that refers to a context menu link. Can be `logs`, `hosts`, `traces`, `profiles`, `processes`, `containers`, or `rum`.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the widget.

fields.name: events
fields.description: List of widget events. Deprecated - Use `overlay` request type instead.

children.name: tags_execution
children.description: The execution method for multi-value filters.

fields.name: legend_columns
fields.description: Columns displayed in the legend.

fields.name: legend_layout
fields.description: Layout of the legend.

fields.name: legend_size
fields.description: Available legend sizes for a widget. Should be one of "0", "2", "4", "8", "16", or "auto".

fields.name: markers
fields.description: List of markers.

children.name: display_type
children.description:
Combination of:
  - A severity error, warning, ok, or info
  - A line type: dashed, solid, or bold
In this case of a Distribution widget, this can be set to be `percentile`.

children.name: label
children.description: Label to display over the marker.

children.name: value
children.description:
Value to apply. Can be a single value y = 15 or a range of values 0 < y < 10.
For Distribution widgets with `display_type` set to `percentile`, this should be
a numeric percentile value (for example, "90" for P90).

fields.name: requests
fields.description: List of timeseries widget requests.

children.name: interval
children.description: Define a time interval in seconds.

children.name: group_by
children.description: List of tag prefixes to group by in the case of a cluster check.

children.name: limit
children.description: Maximum number of items in the group.

children.name: index
children.description: A coma separated-list of index names. Use "*" query all indexes at once. [Multiple Indexes](https://docs.datadoghq.com/logs/indexes/#multiple-indexes)

children.name: multi_compute
children.description: This field is mutually exclusive with `compute`.

children.description: If true, the value is displayed as a label relative to the data point.

children.name: order_by
children.description:
How to order series in timeseries visualizations.
- `tags`: Order series alphabetically by tag name (default behavior)
- `values`: Order series by their current metric values (typically descending)

children.name: palette
children.description: Color palette to apply to the widget.

fields.name: right_yaxis
fields.description: Axis controls for the widget.

children.name: label
children.description: The label of the axis to display on the graph. Only usable on Scatterplot Widgets.

children.name: max
children.description: Specifies maximum numeric value to show on the axis. Defaults to `auto`.

children.name: min
children.description: Specifies minimum numeric value to show on the axis. Defaults to `auto`.

children.name: scale
children.description: Specifies the scale type. Possible values are `linear`, `log`, `sqrt`, and `pow##` (for example `pow2` or `pow0.5`).

fields.name: show_legend
fields.description: (screenboard only) Show the legend for this widget.

fields.name: time
fields.description: Time setting for the widget.

fields.name: hide_incomplete_cost_data
fields.description: Whether to hide incomplete cost data in the widget.

fields.description: Start time in milliseconds since epoch.

fields.name: hide_incomplete_cost_data
fields.description: Whether to hide incomplete cost data in the widget.

fields.description: Background color of the widget. Supported values are `white`, `blue`, `purple`, `pink`, `orange`, `yellow`, `green`, `gray`, `vivid_blue`, `vivid_purple`, `vivid_pink`, `vivid_orange`, `vivid_yellow`, `vivid_green`, and `transparent`.

fields.name: widgets

children.name: layout
children.description: The layout for a widget on a `free` or **new dashboard layout** dashboard.

children.name: height
children.description: The height of the widget. Should be a non-negative integer.

children.name: is_column_break
children.description:
Whether the widget should be the first one on the second column in high density or not.
**Note**: Only for the **new dashboard layout** and only one widget in the dashboard should have this property set to `true`.

children.name: width
children.description: The width of the widget. Should be a non-negative integer.

children.name: x
children.description: The position of the widget on the x (horizontal) axis. Should be a non-negative integer.

children.name: y
children.description: The position of the widget on the y (vertical) axis. Should be a non-negative integer.

entries.code:
# Set your Datadog site and credentials
export DD_SITE="datadoghq.com"
export DD_API_KEY="<DD_API_KEY>"
export DD_APP_KEY="<DD_APP_KEY>"

curl -X GET "https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v1/dashboard/${DASHBOARD_ID}" \
-H "DD-API-KEY: ${DD_API_KEY}" \
-H "DD-APPLICATION-KEY: ${DD_APP_KEY}" \
-H "Accept: application/json"

entries.code:
"""
Get a dashboard returns "OK" response
"""

from os import environ
from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v1.api.dashboards_api import DashboardsApi

# there is a valid "dashboard" in the system
DASHBOARD_ID = environ["DASHBOARD_ID"]

configuration = Configuration()
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = DashboardsApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.get_dashboard(
        dashboard_id=DASHBOARD_ID,
    )

    print(response)

# Get a dashboard returns "OK" response

require "datadog_api_client"
api_instance = DatadogAPIClient::V1::DashboardsAPI.new

# there is a valid "dashboard" in the system
DASHBOARD_ID = ENV["DASHBOARD_ID"]
p api_instance.get_dashboard(DASHBOARD_ID)
